summary: Verified the fix for the 3Dmol.js/React removeChild NotFoundError in Complex3DViewer (DockingViewer.jsx). Ran heavy Playwright navigation stress tests capturing page.on('pageerror') and console.error across the required route cycle. Zero removeChild / NotFoundError entries observed across all runs. Complex3DViewer now uses a dedicated hostRef sibling div (data-testid=viewer-3dhost-<pairId>) that React never renders JSX children into, and the useEffect cleanup manually detaches hostRef's children after calling viewer.clear() — matching the documented fix.
critical_code_review_comments: DockingViewer.jsx L317-333: fix correctly implemented — outer wrapRef div holds the {loading && ...} overlay as a proper React child, and a sibling <div ref={hostRef} data-testid=viewer-3dhost-{pairId} /> is the exclusive 3Dmol mount point with NO JSX children. Confirmed no innerHTML='' calls on React-managed nodes.
useEffect cleanup (L178-194): safely calls viewer.clear() then loops removeChild on hostRef.firstChild only — this is safe because hostRef has no React-tracked children.
fullscreen() and snapshot() correctly prefer hostRef.current and fall back to wrapRef.current — matches spec.

Minor (not a bug): applyStyle is in the useEffect dependency array (L195) which is defined via useCallback with deps [style, showHbondLabels, interactions]. This means changing style/showHbondLabels/interactions will re-run the whole effect (re-fetch complex.pdb + rebuild viewer). Not related to this bug, but could be optimised later by separating the initial-load effect from the applyStyle effect.
seed_data_creation: None — Complex3DViewer only mounts when a docking job/pair is in state. The acceptance test explicitly states this is fine; guarded redirect card on /molecular-docking with no seed is expected behaviour. No runtime errors is the accept criterion.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix in DockingViewer.jsx confirmed working. Test methodology: use page.on('pageerror') + page.on('console') filtered for /removeChild|NotFoundError/ regex, then navigate Home → Molecular Docking → Network Analysis → Molecular Docking → Home in a loop of 3 (via page.goto with wait_until='domcontentloaded'). Also ran rapid 6x flip of {/molecular-docking, /network-analysis, /} with 400ms delays and 3 rounds of SPA <a href> nav clicks. All three stress patterns yielded 0 errors, 0 pageerrors, 0 console.errors. Docking page renders a guarded redirect card when there is no active docking job in state — no Complex3DViewer mount occurs on that path, which is expected.

rca of the issue.root_cause: Complex3DViewer had 3Dmol.js mount its WebGL canvas + label DOM nodes into the SAME React-managed wrapRef div that also contained a conditional {loading && <overlay/>} React JSX child. The code then did wrapRef.current.innerHTML = '' before creating the 3Dmol viewer — this wiped React's tracked overlay node out of the DOM without React knowing. When the component later unmounted (route change / expand-toggle / re-render), React's fiber tree tried removeChild on the overlay node it thought was still there, which had already been evicted → NotFoundError.
rca of the issue.reproduction_steps: Before the fix: (1) navigate to /molecular-docking with a seeded docking job so Complex3DViewer mounts and 3Dmol calls innerHTML='' on wrapRef; (2) navigate away (e.g. to /network-analysis or click Collapse) — React would throw 'Failed to execute removeChild on Node' during commitDeletionEffectsOnFiber.
rca of the issue.mitigation_applied: 1) Introduced a dedicated hostRef div (React never renders JSX children into it) sibling to the loading overlay inside wrapRef. 2) Removed the wrapRef.current.innerHTML='' call. 3) Added a useEffect cleanup return that calls viewer.clear() then manually detaches hostRef.firstChild in a loop, then nulls viewerRef.current — safe because only 3Dmol appends to hostRef. 4) fullscreen()/snapshot() prefer hostRef, fall back to wrapRef.
rca of the issue.verification: Playwright stress test with pageerror + console.error listeners over 3 loops of 5-route cycle + 6 rapid flips + 3 SPA-link rounds → 0 removeChild/NotFoundError events.
